street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:おくししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:おくししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:おくししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:おくししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines {{DEFAULTSORT:おくししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:pages]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市葵区紺屋町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小梳神社]] {{wikidata link|Q134930718}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ogushi Shrine |画像 = [[File:小梳神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}} Kōya-machi 7-13 |位置 = {{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建 = |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 27 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Ogushi Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|58|24.8|N|138|23|10.8|E}}}} }} '''Ogushi Shrine''' (おぐしじんじゃ) is a shrine in Kōya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aoi-ku|Q1131483|WD=Q1131483|1=Aoi-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines. === Overview === It is enshrined just northwest of the north exit of JR {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Station|Q801440|WD=Q801440|1=Shizuoka Station}}, on the north side along Gofuku-chō-dōri. Its foundation is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with a same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}[https://humizuki.com/omaturi_zanmai/sight/jinja/sizuoka/ogusi.htm 小梳神社] - Omatsuri Zanmai[https://genbu.net/data/suruga/ogusi_title.htm 小梳神社] - Genshōshi. It is also called Shōshōinomiya and was revered by the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imagawa clan|1=UNKNOWN}}[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/t/oogosho400/study/07_05.htm 紺屋町と少将井社〔小梳神社〕と代官屋敷] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau. It is also known as a shrine where the young {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Takechiyo) prayed for lasting martial fortune. It once stood at the castle deputy’s residence in the third bailey of {{ill|lt=Sunpu Castle|Q1038629|WD=Q1038629|1=Sunpu Castle}} (present Shizuoka City History Museum, formerly Aoba Elementary School), but with expansion of Sunpu Castle in the Edo period it was moved via Araya-machi (present Miyuki-chō to Tenma-chō) to its current site (Kōya-machi)[https://www.visit-shizuoka.com/spots/detail.php?kanko=543#:~:text=%E5%BE%B3%E5%B7%9D%E5%AE%B6%E5%BA%B7%E3%81%AE%E5%B9%BC%E5%B0%91%E6%9C%9F,%E5%BA%87%E8%AD%B7%E3%82%92%E5%8F%97%E3%81%91%E3%81%BE%E3%81%97%E3%81%9F%25E3%2580%2582 小梳神社] - Suruga Planning and Tourism Bureau[http://www.ieyasu-net.com/shiseki/shizuoka/07shizuokashi/0002.html 小梳神社] - Nationwide Ieyasu Network. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}}), it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takehayasusanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q505469|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kushinadahime}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4406004 小梳神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ogushi Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河区の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:祇園神社]]
